phase1.explanation:
Abstract classes are partial blueprints:

abstract keyword:
• Can't be instantiated (no 'new Shape()')
• Can have abstract methods (no body)
• Can have concrete methods (with body)
• Can have fields and constructors

Abstract methods:
• Declared without body: abstract double area();
• Must be implemented by non-abstract subclasses
• Used to define a contract

When to use abstract classes:
• Share common code among subclasses
• Template for subclasses
• Partial implementation

Key difference from interfaces:
• Abstract class can have state (fields)
• Abstract class can have implementation

Subclasses MUST implement ALL abstract methods (unless they're abstract too).

phase2.errorMessage:
Circle.java:10: error: Circle is not abstract and does not override abstract method area() in Shape
class Circle extends Shape {
^
1 error
phase2.explanation:
Circle doesn't implement the abstract method area() from Shape.

A non-abstract subclass MUST implement ALL abstract methods from its parent.

Options:
1. Implement area() in Circle
2. Make Circle abstract (but then you can't instantiate it)

The compiler forces you to implement all abstract methods!

phase3.prompt:
Create an abstract class Appliance with:
• Field: brand (String)
• Constructor
• Concrete method: turnOn() — prints "[brand] is turning on"
• Abstract method: operate()

Create WashingMachine and Microwave subclasses:
• Each implements operate() with their own behavior
• WashingMachine: prints "[brand] is washing clothes"
• Microwave: prints "[brand] is heating food"

In main, create an array of Appliance and call both methods on each.
dialogueHints: Appliance is abstract — it can have concrete methods too!
